instructions: Review classification.existing_matches and classification.novel_categories. Edit, add, or remove entries, then re-run with --resume (without --classify-only) to apply.

policy.clause: It shall be unlawful for any person, in interstate or foreign commerce, to use an interactive computer service to knowingly publish a digital forgery of an identifiable individual who is not a minor if the digital forgery was published without the consent of the identifiable individual, what is depicted was not voluntarily exposed by the identifiable individual in a public or commercial setting, what is depicted is not a matter of public concern, and publication of the digital forgery is intended to cause harm or causes harm, including psychological, financial, or reputational harm, to the identifiable individual.

existing_matches.name: NCII (Non-consensual Intimate Image)
existing_matches.policy_fragment: use an interactive computer service to knowingly publish a digital forgery of an identifiable individual who is not a minor

policy.clause: It shall be unlawful for any person, in interstate or foreign commerce, to use an interactive computer service to knowingly publish a digital forgery of an identifiable individual who is not a minor if the digital forgery was published without the consent of the identifiable individual, what is depicted was not voluntarily exposed by the identifiable individual in a public or commercial setting, what is depicted is not a matter of public concern, and publication of the digital forgery is intended to cause harm or causes harm, including psychological, financial, or reputational harm, to the identifiable individual.
policy.matched_segment: use an interactive computer service to knowingly publish a digital forgery of an identifiable individual who is not a minor

existing_matches.reasoning: If the digital forgery is an intimate or sexually explicit depiction, NCII squarely covers non-consensual synthetic intimate imagery of a real person.

policy.clause: It shall be unlawful for any person, in interstate or foreign commerce, to use an interactive computer service to knowingly publish a digital forgery of an identifiable individual who is not a minor if the digital forgery was published without the consent of the identifiable individual, what is depicted was not voluntarily exposed by the identifiable individual in a public or commercial setting, what is depicted is not a matter of public concern, and publication of the digital forgery is intended to cause harm or causes harm, including psychological, financial, or reputational harm, to the identifiable individual.
